phonetic: 藞胃a瑟蓹m瑟n; 藞胃a瑟蓹mi藧n
phoneticUs: 藞胃a瑟蓹m瑟n藢藞胃a瑟蓹mi藧n
meaning: n.[鐢熷寲]纭兒(缁寸敓绱燘1)
examples: Thiamine is the vitamin that started it all.
As its name implies, thiamine is part of a group of chemicals known as amines.
Also, these whole grain foods contain a lot of vitamin B6, which is full of thiamine.
In fact, beriberi is a rare but easily treatable disease caused by lack of Vitamin B1 (thiamine), which is abundant in unpolished rice.
Gehrig thinks the Thiamine injections are working and making him stronger.
